s.m. Ornament, the ornament of anything (used in comp. to express greatness or distinction, e.g. trailokya-tilak, 'the ornament of the three worlds,' i.e. a distinguished personage); an ornament or other mark between the eyes or rising between the brows; a sectarial mark or marks made with coloured eye-earths, sandal wood, or unguents upon the forehead and between the eyebrows (as a ceremonial mark it is made on the occasions of installation to office, coronation of a king, betrothal, &c., and hence is sometimes synonymous with 'installation,' 'consecration,' &c.); a freckle, mole, natural mark under the skin ( = til); a species of tree with beautiful flowers; a commentary ( = ṭīkā)
more: n. m. 1. A sectarial mark or marks made with colored unguents on the forehead between the eyebrows. 1. Bhaṛ-bhūnjan kī beṭī, aur kesar kā tilak! Prov. A grain-parcher's daughter black, and saffron on her forehead! 2. Karnī ho, ki nā, re bhāī, Mālā, tilak kī bahut baṛāī! Kabīr. Be good your deeds or no, my friend, Of beads and paint vain boast no end! 2. Installation; consecration ( ṭīkā , 9). 3. A gold or silver ornament worn on the forehead. 4. Certain presents or dues in money or goods made after betrothal to the intended bridegroom by the father of the bride. tilak dhārnā, dhāran k., chaṛhānā, yā lagānā , v. n. To wear, or invest one with the tilak. Tilak lagāe, Gangā nhāe, gal bich ḍāle ḍorā, Kāṅdhe pe ek dhotī gīlī, chhīn līyā man morā, Jāne kabloṅ pher milegā. Chhallā . Painted his brow, in Ganges bathed, thrown round his neck the sacred string, His loin-strip wet his shoulders flu…
s. m. A mark or marks made with coloured earth or unguents upon the forehead and between the eyebrows, either as an ornament or a sectarial distinction.
subs: (-par), tilak dhārnā, tilak dhāran karnā, To form the tilak mark on the forehead, to ornament, to wear the tilak
s.m. One who is marked with the tilak, one who wears the tilak
(-par), To mark with the tilak, to ornament; to anoint, install, consecrate, crown; to betroth; to bid adieu (to)

The master o'er scant oil doth rue, the servant over oil cakes few. (The more oil the fewer cakes, and vice versā ). 2. An unclean or dirty fellow. telī taṅbolī , n. m. Low caste people; the dregs of society. telī rājā , n. m. 1. A sect of mendicants who beg for oil which they put on their heads and clothes. 2. See telī , 2. telī kā bail , n. m. 1. The oilman's ox. 1. Ādmī hai, yā telī kā bail? Phabtī . Chaff. Is he a man, or an oilman's ox? (He is made to work night and day). 2. Telī ke bail ko ghar hī kos pachās . Prov. The st…

def: adj. Three
more: adj. S. त्रि, Pr. tinai . Three 1. Tīn meṅ, na terah meṅ, na ser bhar sutlī meṅ . Prov. Nor 'mongst the three, nor 'mongst thirteen, nor in the seer of string. ☞ The story goes that a certain fille de joie used to classify her admirers as above. The new arrival was neither among the three favorites of the first degree, nor among the thirteen next, nor even among the knotted strings which stood for those who held the lowest place in her affections. tīn pāṅch , n. f. lit . Mujh se hī tīn pāṅch na lānā! tīn titālā , The number three; an odd number. Tīn titālā, chauthe kā mūṅh kālā . Boys' game We three are harmony, a black-faced rogue the fourth whoe'er he be. (Three are company, but four are not.) tīn terah , adj. See bārā bāṭ , 1, 5. 1. Three a…

To startle or shy (a horse). 2. To excite the sensual appetite. bhāṛe kā ṭaṭṭū , n. m. See under bhāṛā. bhāṛe kā ṭaṭṭū , adj. Mercenary; sordid; avaricious.
s. m. Reality, the essential, essence, element, principle. See تت tat.

def: n. f. S. तिथि A lunar day; a date.
more: adj. S. तथ्य True; right; perfect.

def: s.f. A lunar day, the thirtieth part of a whole lunation; day (of the month), date ( = tārīḵẖ)
subs: s.m. The day of new moon; the day or space in which a tithi begins or ends without one sun-rising; the difference between the solar and lunar days in a yug
s.m. The aggregate of the tithis
s.m. Any book containing an account of the tithis.

def: s.f. A sound uttered in calling fowls.
The sand-piper, Tringa goensis; the pewit or lapwing, Tringa vanellus; — a kind of rattle (a child's toy)
subs: kahīṅ ṭaṭīhrī -se āsmān thaṅbegā or thāmā-jāʼegā

'Will the sky ever be supported by the sand-piper?' (said of a person who undertakes an enterprise far above his strength: the sand-piper is fabled to sleep with its legs upwards, as if to prop the firmament).

def: s.f. = ṭiṭīhrī, q.v.
s.f. = ṭiṭīhrī, q.v.
s.f. Endurance, patience, resignation, suffering, forbearance.
part. adj. Borne, endured, suffered; bearing, &c = titikshu, q.v. adj. Bearing, enduring patiently, patient; resigned, forbearing.
adj. Bearing, enduring patiently, patient; resigned, forbearing.
s.m. A sort of sweetmeat made of sesamum ground and baked with sugar (cf. til); — one of the seven karaṇs or astronomical periods.
s.f. A butterfly; (fig.) a grandly dressed woman; a belle; — a species of rue; a medicine given in intermittent fever (in these senses perhaps connected with the S. tikta; cf. titrī; tītrī).
ex:
لمحے اُڑتے ہیں کبھی یا تو تتلیوں کی طرح
یا کبھی خوشبوؤں کی مانند چیخ اُٹھتے ہیں

Moments fly away, sometimes like butterflies,
or sometimes they scream like fragrances.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi تیتری (tytry /⁠tītrī⁠/) / تتری (titrī), from Prakrit 𑀢𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀭𑀺 (tittiri), ultimately from Sanskrit तित्तिर (tittirá). Dasa, Qureshi relate it to تِیتَر (tītar, “partridge”). Compare Punjabi تِتْلِی (titlī) / ਤਿਤਲੀ (titlī), Bengali তিতলি (titli).

def: s.f. = titilī, q.v. s.f. A butterfly; (fig.) a grandly dressed woman; a belle; — a species of rue; a medicine given in intermittent fever (in these senses perhaps connected with the S. tikta; cf. titrī; tītrī).
s.m. Urging on an animal by clacking the tongue (see ṭiṭkārī).
s.f. The clacking of the tongue by placing it on the roof of the mouth and drawing it sharply forward (a sound made to urge horses, &c., to proceed or to move faster)
more: s. f. The noise made by drawing the tongue from the roof of the mouth, to make horses, &c. proceed.
subs: (-kī), To come at a call or sign (from), to be at the beck or call (of); to be domesticated or tame.

def: n. f. The sound made by drawing the tongue from the roof of the mouth to urge horses, etc. to move on or go faster. Bail sarkārī, aur yāroṅ kī ṭiṭkārī . Prov. It's the Government cattle, at pleasure drive! ṭiṭkārī par lagnā , v. n. 1. To come at a whistle, or the slightest sign; to be at one's beck and call ( āvāz par lagnā ). 2. To be tamed.
v.t. To urge on an animal by clacking the tongue.
more: v. a. To urge an animal by ṭiṭkārī, q. v.
